{{Quote|I trust Ka'Pa exactly as far as I can throw him. And I can't.|Kyle Katarn|<ref name="Hutt! Hutt! Hutt!" />}}
'''Ka'Pa''' was a [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]], and crime rival to [[Takara]].

==Biography==
Like many [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lords]] of his kind, Ka'Pa employed numerous [[Gamorrean/Legends|Gamorrean]] guards and posted them throughout his throne room. He also had a [[Protocol droid/Legends|protocol droid]] handy, which was common for Hutts.<ref name="JK: MotS">''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Mysteries of the Sith]]''</ref>

During the waning days of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Old Republic]], Ka'Pa owned a [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] dancing girl named [[B'inka Fibuna]]. She escaped his clutches, earning a live [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] on her head, which was eventually claimed by [[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]].<ref name="Star Wars: Bounty Hunter">''[[Star Wars: Bounty Hunter]]''</ref>

Ka'Pa was involved in several criminal activities such as [[Slavery/Legends|slavery]], [[Spice/Legends|spice]] trading, and arms dealing. After the [[Death/Legends|death]] of [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba the Hutt]] by [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] and company, Ka'Pa mistrusted the [[New Jedi Order]], but established a business relationship with the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]—one of the only Hutts to do so.<ref name="JK: MotS" />

===New Republic entanglements===
Ka'Pa was offered amnesty by the New Republic in exchange for supplying them with resources. [[Mara Jade Skywalker|Mara Jade]] was sent to negotiate; however, due to Ka'Pa's prejudice against the Jedi, she was denied entrance and had to break into [[Ka'Pa's palace|his palace]] and "negotiate" by force.<ref name="JK: MotS" />

[[File:Confronting_kapa.jpg|thumb|left|Ka'Pa confronted by [[Mara Jade Skywalker|Mara Jade]]]]

Ka'Pa asked her to bring him back [[Takara]]'s [[Global Communications Transponder]] device in order to help the Republic. After Mara succeeded and gave the device to Ka'Pa, the Hutt offered the supplies as a gift, in return for Mara's silence: the device was illegal, and Mara, being an honest Jedi, would have had to report its exsistence to [[Mon Mothma/Legends|Mon Mothma]].<ref name="JK: MotS" />

Ka'Pa maintained contact with Mara Jade as well as [[Kyle Katarn]], who had been a longtime contact of Ka'Pa's, both of whom served as the Hutt's liaison with the New Republic.<ref name="Hutt! Hutt! Hutt!" />

===Odd celebrity===
{{Quote|Everyone in the galaxy uses that same image of Jabba, and we're sick of it.|Riccix editors|<ref name="Hutt! Hutt! Hutt!" />}}
Ka'Pa was somewhat of a celebrity, because ''[[Riccix's Revised Basic Holodictionary]]'' featured an image of him in the entry on Hutts. The image was taken by the famous [[Holographer/Legends|holographer]] [[Bovit Leebo]], who was soon killed by Ka'Pa. When Leebo died, the image fell into the public domain, and was quickly snatched up by the editors at [[Riccix]].<ref name="Hutt! Hutt! Hutt!" />

==Behind the scenes==
Ka'Pa the Hutt was voiced by [[Roger Jackson]].<ref name="JK: MotS" /> According to [[Ryan Kaufman (real person)|Ryan Kaufman]], Ka'Pa was created as an original character because the stories of the Hutts already in the [[Canon|continuity]] "were a bit muddy and hard to pin down." His [[Huttese/Legends|Huttese]] dialogue was specially written by [[Ben Burtt]].<ref name="BTS Blog">{{Blog|RyanKaufman|4|Kyle and Mara: Making Mysteries of the Sith - Part II: Zombies of the Dark Side}}</ref>
